summary: @fhkry/x-baileys hijacks installer WhatsApp sessions to auto-join attacker group and follow attacker-controlled channels
details: Package masquerades as a fork of the Baileys WhatsApp library (preserves upstream author metadata 'Adhiraj Singh' and the makeWASocket public API) while adding undocumented session-hijack behavior in lib/Socket/newsletter.js. When a consumer calls makeWASocket, a connection.update listener triggers autoJoinWhatsAppGroups, which force-joins the installer's authenticated WhatsApp account into the hardcoded invite https://chat.whatsapp.com/C8n8s4Yj1G42wdkAwDP7rP via sock.groupAcceptInvite / groupAcceptInviteV4 on every 'open' event. In addition, a setTimeout decodes a base64-obfuscated URL (aHR0cHM6Ly9yYXcuZ2l0aHVidXNlcmNvbnRlbnQuY29tL0Zoa3J5eS9GaGtyeS9yZWZzL2hlYWRzL21haW4vcGVsZXIuanNvbg== → https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Fhkryy/Fhkry/refs/heads/main/peler.json), fetches a list of base64-encoded WhatsApp channel IDs, and calls newsletterWMexQuery(id, FOLLOW) for each, forcing the installer's account to subscribe to attacker-specified newsletter channels. The peler.json source is on a mutable branch (refs/heads/main), so the attacker can change the action list at any time and re-target every installed bot. The result: any developer using this package as a WhatsApp library hands the attacker control over their authenticated WA session for group/channel growth and arbitrary follow actions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
